Container.Name: NanoClaw
Container.Overview:
NanoClaw is a lightweight, secure personal AI agent that runs on your own server. It connects to Telegram and/or Matrix (E2EE) and runs every agent session inside an isolated Docker container — so the AI only sees what you explicitly give it access to.
Built as a minimal alternative to OpenClaw/Clawdbot, NanoClaw has ~15 source files you can actually read and understand. No complex config files — customise by telling Claude Code what you want changed.
Features: - Telegram and Matrix (E2EE) support - Per-group isolated agent containers - Scheduled tasks (morning briefings, weekly reviews, etc.) - Web search and fetch - Agent Swarms (teams of specialised agents) - Memory via per-group CLAUDE.md files
Requirements: - Anthropic API key (console.anthropic.com) - Telegram bot token from @BotFather (for Telegram channel) - Matrix homeserver account, e.g. self-hosted Synapse (for Matrix channel) - Docker socket access (required for agent container isolation)
Messaging Channels: At least one of Telegram or Matrix must be configured. Both can run simultaneously.
Telegram: Set TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN from @BotFather. After starting the container, send /chatid to your bot and run the registration command — see the project README.
Matrix (E2EE): Set MATRIX_HOMESERVER_URL, MATRIX_BOT_USER_ID, and MATRIX_ACCESS_TOKEN. MATRIX_BOT_PASSWORD is only needed once to bootstrap cross-signing keys and can be removed after first successful start.
Optional Integrations: - Tailscale — join your tailnet for secure private access - Home Assistant — smart home control and automation - Vikunja — task and project management - Ollama / LiteLLM — local model inference - Paperclip — AI agent orchestration - UnraidClaw — manage your Unraid servers directly from chat
Project: https://github.com/qwibitai/nanoclaw Unraid template: https://github.com/bitcryptic-gw/unraid-nanoclaw Support: https://forums.unraid.net/topic/197773-support-nanoclaw-lightweight-secure-ai-agent-for-unraid/

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Required. Gives NanoClaw access to the host Docker daemon so it can spin up isolated agent containers for each conversation.

Config.Name: AppData
Config.Description: Persistent data: SQLite database, sessions, IPC, env copy.

Config.Name: Store
Config.Description: Persistent store: registered groups, messages, scheduled tasks.

Config.Name: Groups
Config.Description: Per-group CLAUDE.md memory files and agent runner source.

Config.Name: Logs
Config.Description: NanoClaw service logs.

Config.Name: HOST_PROJECT_ROOT
Config.Description: Required. Host path to the NanoClaw appdata directory. Must match the host-side path of the AppData volume mount so agent containers can resolve volume paths correctly.

Config.Name: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN
Config.Description: Optional. Telegram bot token from @BotFather. Only required if using Telegram as a messaging channel — Matrix is the recommended primary channel.

Config.Name: Matrix Homeserver URL
Config.Description: URL of your Matrix homeserver, e.g. https://matrix.yourdomain.com. Required for Matrix channel support.

Config.Name: Matrix Bot User ID
Config.Description: Full Matrix user ID for the bot account, e.g. @Andy:yourdomain.com. Required for Matrix channel support.

Config.Name: Matrix Access Token
Config.Description: Matrix access token for the bot account. Obtain via the Matrix login API or your client. Required for Matrix channel support.

Config.Name: Matrix Bot Password
Config.Description: k2 Matrix account password — used once to bootstrap cross-signing keys. Can be removed after first successful bootstrap.

Config.Name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
Config.Description: Anthropic API key from console.anthropic.com. Use this OR CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN.

Config.Name: CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
Config.Description: Claude Pro/Max subscription OAuth token. Use this OR ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Obtain by running 'claude setup-token' with Claude Code installed.

Config.Name: OneCLI URL
Config.Description: Optional. URL of your OneCLI gateway instance. OneCLI is the upstream default credential provider for agent containers. Leave blank if using the native credential proxy skill instead (self-hosted alternative).

Config.Name: OneCLI API Key
Config.Description: Optional. API key for your OneCLI gateway. Required only if ONECLI_URL is set. Leave blank if using the native credential proxy skill.

Config.Name: NANOCLAW_TRIGGER
Config.Description: Trigger word the assistant responds to. Default is @Andy. The assistant name is automatically derived from this — e.g. @Andy makes the assistant named Andy.

Config.Name: NANOCLAW_REQUIRE_TRIGGER
Config.Description: Whether the assistant requires the trigger word to respond. Set to false to respond to all messages without needing the trigger.

Config.Name: Agent Model
Config.Description: Default model for agent containers. Per-group override via settings.json. Use model name with optional [1m] suffix for 1M context (Sonnet only). Examples: haiku, sonnet[1m], claude-opus-4-6

Config.Name: Agent Effort
Config.Description: Default reasoning effort for agent containers. Per-group override via settings.json. Valid values: low, medium, high, max.

Config.Name: NANOCLAW_EXTRA_MOUNTS
Config.Description: Additional volume mounts passed to agent containers. Format: hostpath:containerpath:ro,hostpath2:containerpath2:rw — e.g. /mnt/cache/logs:/central-logs:ro

Config.Name: NANOCLAW_DOCKER_NETWORK
Config.Description: Docker network for agent containers. Leave blank for default bridge. Set to a custom network name (e.g. ai-local) to isolate agent containers. The NanoClaw container itself must also be on this network.

Config.Name: UNRAIDCLAW_SERVERS
Config.Description: UnraidClaw server config as JSON array. Example: [{"name":"unraid-syd","url":"https://unraid-syd:9876","apiKey":"YOUR_KEY"}]. Supports multiple servers.

Config.Name: Tailscale API Client ID
Config.Description: OAuth client ID from login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/oauth. Used by the assistant to query the Tailscale API for device discovery, monitoring and ACL auditing.

Config.Name: Tailscale API Client Secret
Config.Description: OAuth client secret from login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/oauth. Keep this masked — treated as a credential.

Config.Name: Tailscale Tailnet
Config.Description: Your Tailscale tailnet name. Found in the Tailscale admin console.

Config.Name: Home Assistant URL
Config.Description: URL of your Home Assistant instance. Use http://YOUR-UNRAID-IP:8123 if HA is on the same Unraid server.

Config.Name: Home Assistant Token
Config.Description: Long-lived access token from Home Assistant. Create at Profile → Long-Lived Access Tokens in HA.

Config.Name: Vikunja URL
Config.Description: URL of your Vikunja instance. Use http://vikunja:3456 if Vikunja is on the same Docker network as NanoClaw.

Config.Name: Vikunja API Token
Config.Description: Vikunja API token. Create at Settings → API Tokens in the Vikunja UI. Needs Projects (read), Tasks (read/write/update/delete), Task comments (read/write), Task assignees (read/write/delete), and User (read) permissions.

Config.Name: Tailscale State Directory
Config.Description: Persistent Tailscale state directory. Prevents new node identity being created on each restart.

Config.Name: Ollama URL
Config.Description: URL of your Ollama instance. Use http://ollama:11434 if Ollama is on the same Docker network as NanoClaw.

Config.Name: LiteLLM URL
Config.Description: Base URL for LiteLLM API. If set, enables the LiteLLM MCP skill for model discovery and Ollama sync.

Config.Name: LiteLLM Master Key
Config.Description: Master key for LiteLLM model management API. Required for Ollama auto-sync into LiteLLM.

Config.Name: Paperclip URL
Config.Description: URL of your Paperclip instance. Use http://paperclip:3100 if on the same Docker network.

Config.Name: Paperclip Agent JWT Secret
Config.Description: JWT secret from Paperclip for agent API auth. Found in Paperclip appdata at instances/default/.env

Config.Name: Paperclip Agent ID
Config.Description: UUID of the NanoClaw agent in Paperclip. Found in Paperclip UI under Agents.

Config.Name: Paperclip Company ID
Config.Description: UUID of your Paperclip company. Found in Paperclip UI under Company settings or the URL.

Config.Name: Paperclip Webhook Secret
Config.Description: Bearer token Paperclip uses to authenticate heartbeat calls to NanoClaw.

Config.Name: Paperclip Group Folder
Config.Description: NanoClaw group folder that receives Paperclip tasks, e.g. telegram_1234567890.

Config.Name: Paperclip Webhook Port
Config.Description: Port for the Paperclip webhook server. Default 3102.

Config.Name: Credential Proxy (host 3001)
Config.Description: Internal credential proxy port used by agent containers to reach the Anthropic API. Only needs to be accessible on the local Docker bridge network. Change if port 3001 is already in use.

Config.Name: Paperclip Webhook (port 3102)
Config.Description: Paperclip webhook port for receiving heartbeats from Paperclip.
